symbols: crown placed on Mary's head — her exaltation as Queen of Heaven, the reward for her faithfulness
Christ performing the coronation — all Mary's glory derives from her Son
gold background — the heavenly realm, the uncreated light of divine glory
blue mantle of Mary — heavenly grace and her role as Mother of the Church
ornate vestments — the royal dignity of both Christ the King and Mary the Queen
description: A close-up of a Gothic panel painting with a rich gold background showing Christ crowning the Virgin Mary. Christ, with golden-red hair, a halo, and richly ornamented blue and red vestments, places a small crown on Mary's head with his right hand. Mary bows her head in acceptance, wearing a blue mantle over an ornate robe, her hands clasped in prayer. Both figures have golden halos with decorative patterns. The background features Gothic architectural elements with red and gold decorative patterns. The style is typical of Italian Trecento painting.
theological_description: The Coronation of the Virgin is the culmination of the Marian cycle in Christian art, depicting Mary receiving the crown of glory from her Son in heaven. This devotional subject, while not narrated in Scripture, is grounded in the logic of the Magnificat: 'He has regarded the humble estate of his servant. For behold, from now on all generations will call me blessed' (Luke 1:48). The one who was most humble on earth is most exalted in heaven. Psalm 45:9, 'At your right hand stands the queen in gold of Ophir,' was read by the Church Fathers as referring to Mary's heavenly queenship. Revelation 12:1, the Woman clothed with the sun with a crown of twelve stars, further supports the tradition. The Coronation enacts the promise of James 1:12: 'Blessed is the one who remains steadfast under trial, for when he has stood the test he will receive the crown of life.' Mary, who bore the sword of Simeon's prophecy and stood at the cross, now receives the crown. That Christ himself performs the coronation emphasizes that Mary's glory is entirely derivative — she shines with borrowed light, reflecting the glory of her Son.

scripture_refs.reason: All generations will call me blessed — fulfilled in Mary's heavenly coronation
The queen at the right hand in gold — the royal imagery applied to Mary's heavenly exaltation
The Woman with a crown of twelve stars — the apocalyptic vision behind the Coronation tradition
The crown of life given to those who endure — Mary's faithfulness rewarded with heavenly glory
The crown of righteousness laid up for those who have loved his appearing
